description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly dark gray coloration and subtle blueish undertones. Its surface is rough and uneven, featuring jagged edges and a few flatter areas that suggest it may have been naturally fractured or weathered over time. The stone displays some lighter patches and mottling, likely due to mineral deposits or surface abrasion. One side appears slightly concave, while the rest of the object is more angular and blocky. There is a small, rectangular label affixed to one face, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The overall appearance suggests a dense, solid rock, suitable for use as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description: A dark gray, irregular stone with rough, jagged surfaces and subtle blueish tones, featuring lighter mottling and a catalog label on one side.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's pointed, angular end and the sloping, uneven surface with visible texture.
description_view_1: This side shows the flattest face of the stone, with the catalog label clearly visible and a slightly concave area.
description_view_2: The stone appears more triangular from this angle, emphasizing its blocky mass and mottled surface coloration.
description_view_3: This view reveals the longest, flattest side of the stone, showing its rough, weathered texture and subtle color variations.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and mineral veining with irregular, natural color variations; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly abraded, with some lighter patches indicating moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but could blend with natural landscaping or other stones; may accumulate debris or vegetation at its base.
